mainmatter.p: Let I \subseteq A be a directed subset such that \bigsqcup {I} is positive; we must find a positive element of I. Because A is positively generated we know that \bigsqcup {I} \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left ( I \cap A^+ \right ) \mathclose {}}. Because \bigsqcup {I} is positive and I \cap A^+ is semidirected, it follows that the latter is inhabited.

frontmatter.title: Positively generated dcpos have open positivity predicates

mainmatter.p: Let A be a positively generated pointed dcpo. Then A has an open positivity predicate.
When a pointed dcpo A satisfies either of the following equivalent conditions, we will refer to it as positively generated.

ol.li: Every element a: A is positively generated, i.e. we have a = \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a.
For any semidirected subset I \subseteq A, we have \bigsqcup I = \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}}.

mainmatter.p: (2) implies (1) by setting I = { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \right \} \mathclose {}}. Conversely, assume that each element is the supremum of its positive part and fix a semidirected subset I \subseteq A. We compute:
mainmatter.tex: \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}} = \bigsqcup _{a \in { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}} }a = \bigsqcup _{a \in I} \bigsqcup _{p \in \mathsf { pos } \, a} a = \bigsqcup _{a \in I} a

frontmatter.title: The positive part of a semidirected subset is semidirected

mainmatter.p: Let I \subseteq A be a semidirected subset of a pointed dcpo A; then the intersection I \cap A^+ is semidirected, where A^+ is the subdcpo spanned by positive elements.

Let i,j be two positive elements of I; we must show that there exists a positive element k \in I such that i \sqsubseteq k and j \sqsubseteq k. This follows immediately from the directedness of I and the fact that positivity is monotone.
